rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO GARCIA et al VS. DOHERTY PAINTING & CONSTRUCTION INC A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Fernando And Rafael Garcia For Failure To Comply With San Francisco Local Rules, Ccp 575.2 And Gov'T Code 68600
rulings.Rulings: 1Matter on calendar on Wednesday, November 5, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTs FRANCES DOHERTY and DOHERTY PAINTING & CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Fernando And Rafael Garcia For Failure To Comply With San Francisco Local Rules, Ccp 575.2 And Gov'T Code 68600. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES E. YEAGER et al VS. WILD, CARTER & TIPTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Yeagers Motion Seeking Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, November 5, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFFs CHARLES YEAGER and VICTORIA YEAGER's Motion Seeking Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint. Motion denied without prejudice. Plaintiff fails to explain the delay in bringing its Motion, and none of the declarations provide sufficient detail as to what new facts were discovered, when and how they were discovered, and why there were not previously known or discovered. (See CRC Rule 3.1324.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE BRAIS VS. MEI GIM LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request Fro Production Of Documents, Request For Reasonable Sanctions And Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 5, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MEI LEE, THE MEI GIM LEE REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 9/12/94 Defendants Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents, Request For Reasonable Sanctions And Attorneys Fees. Pro Tem Judge Margaret Niver, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' unopposed motion to compel responses to form interrogatories, special interrogatories and requests for production and for sanctions against plaintiff George Brais is granted. Plaintiff shall serve verified responses, without objection, within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. Sanctions in the amount of $360.00 are awarded against plaintiff, payable to defendants within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Margaret.niver@pillsburylaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN KOOPMAN VS. RED AND WHITE FLEET et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Index Of Evidence; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, November 5, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT RAMOS OIL COMPANY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. The general negligence cause of action is barred by the applicable two-year statute of limitations. (CCP sec. 335.1.) The Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") alleges that Plaintiff was injured on June 8, 2010. (TAC, par. 24.) The original complaint was filed on June 12, 2012, beyond the two-year limitations period. Plaintiff's argument that the negligence cause of action is brought under the Federal Maritime Act is not supported by the allegations pled in the TAC and therefore may not be properly considered on summary judgment. (See Turner v. State of California (1991) 232 Cal.App.3d 883, 891, "The pleadings delimit the issues to be considered on a motion for summary judgment.") Plaintiff's request for leave to amend is denied. The Court previously granted Plaintiff leave to amend after Defendant's first motion for summary judgment was granted. Plaintiff has not established that all the facts giving rise to its negligence claim were not known to it at the time it filed the Third Amended Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
